calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-11-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Mayo appeared by telephone for Lloyd Burnett (Administrator). Regina Rodney in Propria Persona. Christie Mitchell also present.

Ms. Rodney informed the Court that she would not be hiring Counsel. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-15-2010 Continued To Feb-10-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Mayo for and with Lloyd Burnett (appeared by telephone). Tanya Bieche in Propria Persona.

Ms. Mayo stated that she informed Ms. Rodney of the hearing date/time, that everthing had been properly accounted for and administered, and that Ms. Rodney raised an issue on two bank accounts pre-death. In addition, she stated that one account was with Ms. Rodney and administered as Power of Attorney, and that she thought one had her Client's name only but that she was not sure as she was unable to get the bank records. Ms. Beiche stated that the $23,000.00 belonged to her Grandmother, was put in her Father's name/account for the purpose of a higher interest rate and then put back into the Grandmother's account. With regard to the $63,000.00, she stated that the account had been transferred from the Grandmother to her Sister, then put into the Sister's Son's name, that there was a question of elder abuse and that the money had been put back into the Grandmother's account. Mr. Burnett stated that money was spent on repairs to the house, home care, hospital bills, payment to day workers, that he had no bank statements and that he had provided a break down. In addition, he stated that the house was put in his name to prevent probate. Ms. Mayo stated that the $5,000.00 amount was a lien paid to the City and County of San Francisco. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-22-2009 Continued To Jan-06-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-08-2009 Continued To Dec-22-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-17-2009 Continued To Dec-08-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alexis Donoghoe specially appeared for Counsel Mary Mayo for Lloyd Burnett (Petitioner). Regina Rodney (Heir) in Propria Persona. Christie Mitchell (Friend of Ms. Rodney) also present.

Ms. Rodney stated that she had attempted to obtain Counsel but that no one would take the case on pro bono. The Court asked if the parties were interested in mediation. Ms. Donoghoe stated that she was not in a position to mediate and that the Petitioner would be available in December. The matter was continued to December 8, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court strongly encouraged Ms. Rodney to hire an Attorney.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-14-2009 Continued To Nov-17-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Mayo for Lloyd Burnett (Admininstrator). Regina Rodney (Heir) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Mayo stated that she saw the declaration filed by Ms. Rodney, that the information could not be verified and that there were no exhibits. Ms. Rodney stated that she had bank statements and police reports. The Court directed Ms. Rodney to provide copies of said documents to Ms. Mayo. Ms. Rodney expressed concern about how the Administrator was handling the estate funds, withdrawals/transfers made from the joint account she held with her Grandmother and her Grandmother's capacity. The Court suggested mediation and suggested Ms. Rodney seek Counsel. Ms. Mayo stated that she needed documents from the date of death. The matter was continued to November 17, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Jung.
